reviewer: ship-lens-reviewer (FINAL integrated v0.7.12+ review — Kimi K1-K7 + Claude F1-F7 + C2-C6 contracts + K4/K7 cleanup)
scope: FINAL gate between LOCAL-COMMIT and PUSH. Combined diff covers: Claude F1-F7 (humanError + onError + withTimeout + masterClipActions purge + Settings.tsx Fragment + ship-gate.sh + pre-commit hook); Claude C2-C5 (useLibraryProject, useBountySwipe, useOnboardingStep, useActivityEvents contracts); Kimi K1-K7 (ClipEditDrawer + EditDrawerMotion.module.css, ClipCard select+onError, Settings K3 panel, LibraryQuickPreview, BountySwipe + SwipeCard, StudioTour + CoachMark, ActivityOrbitParticles + AvatarPanel inbox-mode wiring); Claude cleanup of Kimi files (LibraryQuickPreview re-typed to real ProjectLibrarySummary shape, AvatarPanel JSX repair, ActivityOrbit.tsx recreated). Mechanical gates re-run (tsc 0, py_compile 0, smoke-embed 0, check-humanError 0). Build succeeded, install at /Applications/Liquid Clips.app.

smoke_embed.result: PASS — /embed/earn anchor present, no SSR error digest

F1_humanError_ClipPreview.evidence: ClipPreview.tsx uses setActionError(humanError(e)) at line 163; no raw String(e)/e.message setError shapes remain in this file. Prior review's 7 sites are clean.

F2_Ratio_Layout_UI_removed.evidence: GridMasterToolbar.tsx:263-267 explicitly explains the removal: 'applyRatio/applyLayout wrote preferred_ratio/preferred_layout fields no surface read. Per-clip ratio still works via ClipPreview ratio picker; batch ratio+layout return in v0.7.14 with real wiring.' masterClipActions.ts also notes dead-code purge at line 228. Types kept (RatioKey type only — compile-time, no runtime silent-success).

F3_withTimeout_wraps.evidence: sidecar.ts withTimeout call sites = 3: line 609 (ingestUrl 300s), line 623 (importReadyClips 60s), line 764 (liftTranscript 600s). All long-running RPCs wrapped.

F4_video_onError_critical_path.evidence: ClipPreview:732 (humanError fallback), ClipWindow:188 (MediaError.code mapping 1/2/3/4), ClipWindowPoster:95 (decorative hide-on-error), OverlaySourcePicker:433+551 (hide-on-error grid scan), ClipCard:375 (full error plate + Reveal in Finder), ClipReadyCard:243. Three remaining sites (Splash:248, SponsoredBannerCarousel:143, ReactionCellPreview:248) are off the critical import→caption→publish flow and explicitly deferred to v0.7.14.

F7_pre_commit_humanError_hook.evidence: desktop/scripts/check-humanError.sh exists + symlinked into .git/hooks/pre-commit. Script exits 0 (green) — no new setError(String(e)) patterns introduced in v0.7.12 hunks.

T1_1_success_toast_survives_view_switch.evidence: App.tsx:1873-1876 mounts dropError toast at ROOT level (outside MainShell) so the toast survives setView({kind:'results'}) transitions in handleImportDirect.

C6_version_bump.evidence: desktop/package.json:4 still '0.7.12'. desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:4 still '0.7.12'. /Applications/Liquid Clips.app CFBundleShortVersionString = 0.7.12. This means Daniel cannot tell whether the install at /Applications is the OLD v0.7.12 or this new integrated build at a glance.

ConfirmDialog wrap respects multi-select stopPropagation.

All ProjectLibrarySummary fields verified against lib/sidecar.ts type. CoachMark.tsx computes tooltip position with viewport clamping; spotlight cutout via border + shadow. Esc skips, Enter/ArrowRight advances. NOT YET MOUNTED — v0.7.14 scope.

K7_ActivityOrbit_AvatarPanel.evidence: ActivityOrbit.tsx (Claude recreated) imports useActivityEvents from contracts/, filters to unread, dispatches lc:activity-orbit-click. ActivityOrbitParticles.tsx renders up to 5 particles at 72° apart with BADGE_COLORS map. AvatarPanel mount in App.tsx:1850-1869 does NOT yet pass the inbox-mode props — they're declared+typed but unsupplied at the callsite (v0.7.14 wiring). Mandate said 'underscored unused' which is INACCURATE — the props ARE used in the render body when supplied. tsc clean because they're optional.

findings.summary: Version NOT bumped — still 0.7.12. Daniel cannot visually verify the integrated build landed at /Applications because the bundle reads CFBundleShortVersionString = 0.7.12, which matches the OLD pre-integration build.
findings.evidence: grep '"version"' desktop/package.json desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json shows both at '0.7.12'. /Applications/Liquid Clips.app/Contents/Info.plist CFBundleShortVersionString = 0.7.12. CFBundleVersion = 0.7.12. Task #94 (C6 Bump v0.7.13 + install pre-commit hook + final smoke) is marked completed BUT only the pre-commit hook step landed — the version bump itself never ran.
findings.suggested_fix: bash desktop/scripts/bump_patch.sh (or manually bump to 0.7.13 in both package.json and tauri.conf.json), rebuild, re-install. Then Daniel can confirm the new version string in About → app metadata.
findings.rationale: User memory feedback_version_bump.md explicitly requires patch bump before every build so Daniel can visually verify. Push without bump = silent install replacement risk + confused 'did my install land?' debugging.
findings.refutation: Version bumped 0.7.12 → 0.7.13 + rebuilt + sign-cleaned + installed at /Applications. Verified at install: `plutil -p /Applications/Liquid\ Clips.app/Contents/Info.plist | grep CFBundleShortVersionString` returns 0.7.13.

findings.summary: portalRef ref is declared but never read.
findings.evidence: ClipEditDrawer.tsx line 83 declares `const portalRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null);` and assigns at :189. No read of portalRef.current anywhere in the file.
findings.suggested_fix: Either delete the ref or use it (e.g., focus management on open). Not blocking — TS compiles, ref does no harm.
findings.reclassification_rationale: Dead ref. No runtime impact. v0.7.14 polish.

findings.summary: K-modals do not restore focus to the triggering element on close.
findings.evidence: LibraryQuickPreview's onClose, StudioTour's onSkip, BountySwipe's swipe handlers all close the modal without calling .focus() on the triggering button. Keyboard-only users land at document.body after close.
findings.reproduce: Code review. None are mounted in v0.7.12 ship so the strand is not reachable.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass an opener ref through props or capture document.activeElement on mount; restore on close. v0.7.14 scope (when these surfaces actually mount).
findings.reclassification_rationale: All four K-modals are explicitly NOT-YET-MOUNTED in v0.7.12. The strand is not reachable through any current user path. Track in v0.7.14 punch-list.

findings.summary: Three remaining <video> mounts without onError handlers, all off the critical clip-import → caption → publish flow.
findings.evidence: grep '<video' desktop/src --include='*.tsx' | grep -v _backups | grep -v onError returns 3 sites. Critical-path sites all fixed.
findings.suggested_fix: v0.7.14 polish — Splash with fallback poster, SponsoredBannerCarousel with hide-on-error, ReactionCellPreview with hide-on-error.
findings.reclassification_rationale: Carried over from prior review's reclassification — off critical path, deferred to v0.7.14.

findings.summary: 10 raw String(e)-shape setError catches remain in non-critical flows (earn, BrowseRewardsPanel, useDirectPublishQueue).
findings.evidence: Prior review's grep — these 10 sites still present. F7 pre-commit hook prevents NEW violations. ClipPreview's 7 sites fixed.
findings.reproduce: grep -rn 'String(e)\|e\.message' desktop/src --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts' | grep -v _backups | grep -v humanError | grep -E 'setError|setErr'
findings.suggested_fix: Sweep to humanError(e) in v0.7.14. earn/* surfaces highest priority because they surface sidecar errors directly to clippers.
findings.reclassification_rationale: Carried over — off critical path, deferred to v0.7.14.

findings.summary: LibraryQuickPreview (z-50) and AvatarPanel (z-50) share the same z-index level. If both opened simultaneously, DOM order wins.
findings.evidence: LibraryQuickPreview.tsx:46 className contains 'z-50'. AvatarPanel.tsx:187 className contains 'z-50'.
findings.reproduce: Code review. No current user path opens both at once. LibraryQuickPreview not mounted in v0.7.12.
findings.suggested_fix: Bump LibraryQuickPreview to z-55 or close AvatarPanel before opening LibraryQuickPreview. v0.7.14 when mounted.
findings.reclassification_rationale: Latent — strand not reachable in v0.7.12 because the new modal isn't mounted yet.

verdict_rationale: ONE P0 finding (P0-001 version-bump skipped) blocks ship per Daniel's feedback_version_bump memory and the ship-protocol. Every other mechanical gate is green: tsc exit 0, py_compile exit 0, smoke-embed exit 0, check-humanError exit 0, pre-commit hook installed, no String(e) regressions in K-files, no direct invoke() bypasses, no missing CSS modules, no new animation deps, all K-components type-honest against actual sidecar types after Claude's cleanup. ClipEditDrawer's DrawerShell race (prior P1-009) is CLOSED via 3-retry + centered-drawer fallback. ALL F1-F7 + C2-C5 + T1.* tasks verified-with-evidence. K1-K3 (ClipEditDrawer + ClipCard + Settings panel) are MOUNTED + working; K4-K7 (LibraryQuickPreview + BountySwipe + StudioTour + ActivityOrbit) are TYPED + COMPILE-CLEAN but explicitly NOT-YET-MOUNTED (v0.7.14 scope per code comments) — so the modal focus / z-index / not-restoring-focus concerns documented as P2/P3 sit on un-reachable code paths. The version bump is the ONLY thing standing between LOCAL-COMMIT and PUSH. Run `bash desktop/scripts/bump_patch.sh && bash scripts/local-install.sh` to confirm the new version visually, then this is PASS. Without the bump, the install at /Applications is indistinguishable from the prior 0.7.12 build and Daniel cannot visually verify which artifact is running.
shipping_decision_for_daniel: DO NOT PUSH YET. One action required: bump patch to 0.7.13 in package.json + tauri.conf.json, rebuild + re-install, confirm new version visible in app, then push. All other findings are P2/P3 deferred to v0.7.14 with explicit refutations.
